rulings.Case Title: DAVID PLEIMANN VS. ROBBY PHILLIPS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Terms Of Stipulation For Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 2, 2024 line 1. Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. rulings.Case Title: FREDERICK O. LEWIS III VS. CIARA KILLBURN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 2, 2024 line 7. DEFENDANT CIARA KILBURN NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is OFF CALENDAR. Parties failed to properly meet and confer. The parties are ordered to meet and confer in person or over the phone as required, before this motion may be re-filed. Moving party shall initiate the meet and confer with the self-represented Plaintiff offering 3 dates and times and the Plaintiff, in pro per, shall respond promptly (within 2 business days) as to the availability. The motion maybe re-filed, if necessary, after meeting and conferring. Plaintiff may also file an amended complaint, if upon meeting a conferring Plaintiff wishes to do so. The Court notes that the moving papers list "July 15, 2024" as a trial date in this action. There is no such trial date in the register of action. The Court further notes that there is no order file (that the Court can locate) regarding the motion to consolidate heard on June 28, 2023 in this action or in case CGC-23-603805. Parties may present a filed endorsed copy of such order at the hearing on May 2, 2024 if they are in possession of such order. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ROGER STERN VS. LENA GIKKAS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 2, 2024 line 8. CROSS DEFENDANT ROGER STERN DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend as to causes of action for breach of contract, constructive trust and fraud. The Amended Cross-Complaint is uncertain as to the terms of the parties' agreements and representations. Cross-Complainant appears to argue both (1) that the term of the agreement was sharing in the proceeds (not defined whether profit only) of a sale at some unidentified time in future per the first cause of action; and (2) that the term of the agreement made the Cross-Complainant an equitable owner in the property before the sale per the cause of action for constructive trust, which is argued to be a remedy available under the first cause of action. Cross-Complainant shall have final leave to amend to allege ALL terms of the agreement and all representations made by the Cross-Defendants. Demurrer to the cause of action of Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress is sustained without leave to amend on statute of limitations grounds. Cross-Complainant failed to address it in opposition, conceding the issue. Demurrer to the causes of action for Quantum Meruit and Unjust Enrichment is sustained as they have been alleged without leave of Court and outside of the scope of the leave to amend granted in conjunction with the prior demurrer. Cross-Complainant shall have (1) 10 days from the notice of entry of order to file an amended cross-complaint with all amendments in bold font; or (2) within 10 days of notice of entry of order Cross-Complainant may file a motion for leave to assert causes of action for quantum meruit and unjust enrichment. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
